heroDesc: Multi-axis milling, turning and complex machining
bodyTitle: CNC Machining for precision metal and plastic functional parts
bodyDesc: CNC machining uses milling, turning, drilling, tapping and multi-axis operations to manufacture precision parts for prototypes, low-volume runs and demanding functional components. Tolerance capability depends on material, size, wall thickness, fixturing and heat treatment, so critical requirements should be specified on a 2D drawing.

panels.title: Process Range
panels.subtitle: Milling / Turning
panels.body: Suitable for hole patterns, cavities, threads, surfaces and assembly datums.

panels.title: Precision Control
panels.subtitle: Datum First
panels.body: Critical dimensions are controlled through fixturing, toolpath and in-process inspection.

panels.title: Finishing
panels.subtitle: Combined as Needed
panels.body: Can pair with anodizing, bead blasting, passivation, black oxide and heat treatment.

tableRows: Hole positions, mating faces, datum surfaces
Tighter tolerances require drawing and process review
Depends on aspect ratio and tool reach
Thin walls may deform during fixturing
Fine machining or polishing can improve finish

steps.title: Drawing and tolerance review
steps.body: Confirm material, critical dimensions, datums, threads and finish requirements.

steps.title: Process and fixture planning
steps.body: Define setups, toolpaths, stock allowance and inspection points.

steps.title: Machining and in-process checks
steps.body: Start stable machining after first-article confirmation.

steps.title: Finishing and delivery
steps.body: Complete deburring, cleaning, finishing, inspection and packaging.

asideCta.title: Need a Manufacturing Plan?
asideCta.body: After drawings are submitted, engineers can review geometry, material and volume to suggest a suitable process route.

related.title: Related Process Recommendations
related.desc: Depending on geometry, material and delivery stage, other processes can be combined into a complete manufacturing flow.

items.title: Complex Part Machining

items.title: Metal 3D Printing
items.body: For internal channels and lightweight structures.

items.title: Surface Finishing
items.body: Improves corrosion resistance, appearance and function.

faq.title: CNC Machining FAQ
faq.desc: These answers help clarify fit, input files and quotation review before submitting a manufacturing request.

items.q: What projects is CNC Machining suitable for?
items.a: CNC Machining is suitable for projects that need manufacturing review based on drawings, material, quantity, tolerances and delivery goals, especially prototypes, low-volume parts and functional components.

items.q: What information is needed for a CNC Machining quote?
items.a: Prepare 3D files, 2D drawings when tolerances matter, material grade, quantity, finish requirements, inspection needs and target delivery timing so engineers can evaluate the route accurately.

items.q: How are lead time and cost confirmed for CNC Machining?
items.a: Lead time and cost are confirmed after reviewing geometry, material, process route, finishing, inspection scope and order quantity. Complex tolerances or special finishes may require engineering confirmation.

ctaDesc: Share drawings and requirements — our engineers will recommend a stable process route.
